Petition For Auhtority And Approval Of Investing Funds In Eft Portfolio
Off Calendar Nov-10-2021 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances: Sheila Robello, via ZOOM, for Barbara Simon, via ZOOM, Trustee.

The court requests clarification from counsel regarding the hedged equity fund. Counsel reports that it is a diversified fund that is schedule to sell if it falls to a certain level and if liquidated, the funds will be distributed to the trust. The court asks for clarification of the costs associated with this investment. Counsel reports that the costs range from .04 percent to .55 percent with differing ranges and a total cost of about .9 percent to manage the entire portfolio.

The Petition for Authority and Approval of Investing Funds in EFT Portfolio is submitted.

Judge: Hon. Ross Moody. Clerk: Robert Woods. Deputy: R. Rosado. Not reported.

REPORT OF SALE AND PETITION TO CONFIRM SALE OF REAL PROPERTY (Report Of Sale And Petition For Order Confirming Sale Of 170 Day St, San Francisco, Ca 94131)

Petition To Approve Settlement Agreement
Off Calendar Sep-24-2018 Advanced From Dec-05-18 To Oct-24-18 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Petition To Approve Settlement Agreement
Off Calendar Oct-31-2018 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Lawrence Siracusa Court appointed Counsel for Conservatee.
Natalie Lupo for Barbara Simon (Conservator).
Franchesca Callejo for John Ferrucci.

Mr. Siracusa stated that she spoke with the Conservatee, that the Conservatee is not opposing the petition and that she confirmed she wanted John to get the house. Mr. Siracusa also stated that the Conservatee feels $100,000.00 is a lot of money, but she understands John needs to get out of the house, that the Conservatee indicated that she has no communication with Joseph and when she does he yells at her, that the funds are coming out of the RAM/pot of the house and that there is no residue. Ms. Callejo stated that there is no residue, that her Client's Brother has had no communication with the family and that she discovered that Mr. Bradshaw has been suspended. Ms. Callejo also stated that Mr. Ferrucci decided to go with her instead, that her understanding is Ms. Robello was not involved in the landlord tenant issue and she does not believe there is an issue with conflict and that she has advised her Client to transfer/deliver the funds to her or to Ms. Simon. Ms. Lupo stated that she can revise the order to reflect how the funds are to be transferred, that Ms. Robello never represented Mr. Bradshaw in any individual capacity what so ever, that there is no residue, that she can amend the settlement agreement and that this does not have to involve Joe. Ms. Callejo stated that her Client is anxious to receive the money, and that he needs the funds to move out of the house and into his new residence. Mr. Siracusa asked that rather than amend the settlement agreement, the Court sign a revised order. The Petition to Approve Settlement Agreement is under submission.

Judge: Andrew Y. S. Cheng. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: R. Rosado. Not reported.

Off Calendar Oct-02-2018 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Sheila Robello and Natalie Lupo for Barbara Simon (Petitioner/Trustee).
Marta Eisenberg Court appointed Counsel in the conservatorship matter for Marilyn Ferrucci (Conservatee/Beneficiary).

Ms. Eisenberg stated that her Client does not have any objections and that she met with her Client, but she does not feel she fully understands. Ms. Eisenberg also stated that the petition makes sense to her and that she does not feel there is anyway around it. Ms. Robello and Ms. Lupo have nothing to add. The Court granted the Petition for Authority to Sell Real Property (subject to review of the order).

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: R. Rosado. Not reported.

Petition For Order Authorizing Successor Trustee To Obtain Reverse Mortgage On Trust Real Property, Or In The Alternative, To Sell The Real Property
Off Calendar Aug-29-2016 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Kevin Lachona for Barbara Simon. Court appointed counsel Marta Eisenberg for Marilyn Ferrucci. Francesca Callejo (via court call) for John Ferrucci.

The Court stated that this is a petition to authorize a reverse mortgage and asked if Ms. Ferrucci is on board with it. Ms. Eisenberg stated that she met with Ms. Ferrucci and that she has no objections. Mr. Lachona reported that it is the intent that Ms. Ferrucci return home, that she has made progress regarding stabilizing and that the home would be the least restrictive environment. Ms. Eisenberg reported that there had been issues of outbursts and aggression but that Ms. Ferrucci seems to be on the correct medication now and is stabilizing. Ms. Callejo stated that she spoke with her client and he has no objections, that he agrees that his mother is doing better and has stabilized, that she has now been properly diagnosed and medicated, that there is still a distinct possibility that she can go home, that Mr. Ferrucci is in agreement, that they do not have a sense of the timing and that she has been at Age Song for a couple of months. Ms. Eisenberg stated that she is not sure of the timetable to return home. Mr. Lachona reported that Ms. Simon says it is difficult to say exactly when she will return home, confirmed that it will be a matter of weeks or months but not years and that the conservatee's son is still living on the property. The Court stated that this seems appropriate, that everyone is on board and granted the petition for order authorizing successor trustee to obtain a reverse mortgage on trust real property (subject to review of the order).

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Grimaldi. (Not Reported)
